sys_product_help.text:
Tasker Library FAQs

1.          What is Tasker?

Tasker is a task management application built on ServiceNow, a cloud-based platform. The application modernizes and streamlines processes within the organization. This tool will allow oversight of an entire organization, ensuring visibility and scalability.

2.          What does Tasker do?

Tasker is a tool that helps you stay productive and on top of all your tasks. You can organize your work visually and automate reports for yourself or others.

3.          What is a task?

A task is an assignment with a deadline given to an individual or group.

4.          What is a template?

A template is a repeatable task or sequence of tasks.

5.          What is the difference between a task and a template?

A task is a single one-time assignment whereas a template is a repeatable set of tasks.

6.          What is an organizational hierarchy?

The organizational hierarchy is an organization chart of the positions in your organization, showing the levels, units, and members.

7.          Can I load the entire hierarchy with a single import?

Not currently. You must load the hierarchy one level at a time. Use the same steps when loading each level.

8.          What is an organizational unit and subunit?

An organizational unit (OU) is a group set to achieve a function or job within an organization. A subunit is a component of a unit.

9.          What is a Tasker OU member?

A person of your organization or team.

10.      What are the minimum system requirements to install?

Please refer to section Pre-Installation Checklist Pre-Installation Checklist for minimum system requirements for installation.

11.      What permissions are needed to install?

You must have admin to install.

12.      What are the required plugins?

You must have the Process Flow Formatter and State Flow plugins.

13.      What permissions should I give my user base?

All system users will have role-based permissions associated with their accounts. Some roles are standard ServiceNow roles, and others are specialized to the organizational unit (OU) or global application. There are six types of permissions:

•             Basic user permissions

•             Application permissions

•             Members permissions

•             Task management permissions

•             Template permissions

•             Organizational hierarchy permissions

14.      How do you add a ServiceNow user to Tasker?

To add a user to Tasker, select the OU to which the user will belong. Add the user to the OU as a new member with the appropriate permissions. If the user us not in ServiceNow, they will not appear in the list lookup. Admins may do this this as well as those with the following permissions as outlined:

•             Global hierarchy administrator: Allows the user to add/update Sub Organizational Units in all OUs, and manage their Members

•             OU hierarchy administrator: Allows the user to add/update Sub Organizational Units, and manage Members in the selected Organizational Unit

•             Member administrator: Allows the user to add/update members for the selected Organizational Unit.

Refer to the section Associate/Link Users to the Hierarchy for more information. Associate/Link Users to the Hierarchy

15.      How should I load my user base?

Only system administrators manage and load the user base. Please refer to associate/link users to the hierarchy for instructions. Associate/Link Users to the Hierarchy

16.      How do I reset a user’s password?

Use the application navigator and go to System Security > Users and Groups > Users. You can manage account lockout and password resets from here.

 17.      How do you remove a Tasker user?

Go to System Security>Users. Select the user and select the roles tab. Remove any Tasker roles. Remember if a member is a part of an active process, we recommend that you do not delete or disable the member. We advise you to first reassign tasks associated with the member before removing or deleting the member. You may also use the context menu from the user's permissions modal. Refer to the section Delete a User. Delete a User

18.     How do I change or customize Tasker's email notifications?

Refer to the section Customize Email Notifications. Customize Email Notifications

19.      What roles and permissions are needed to change the organizational hierarchy?

The role associated with organizational hierarchy permissions is OU Hierarchy Admin. You may add members to the organizational hierarchy as outlined below.

•             OU Hierarchy Admin: Adds, updates, deletes members and sub OUs in the OU

•             Global Hierarchy Admin: Makes changes to the hierarchy at any level

20.      What roles and permissions are needed to create a template?

You must assign a template permission. Please refer to permissions. Permissions in Tasker

21.      What roles and permissions are needed to assign roles and permissions to other users?

Permissions administrators can assign and permissions to other users. Please refer to section on permissions for additional information about permissions administration. Permissions in Tasker

22.      How do you check the roles and permissions assigned to a Tasker user?

Enter Users in the application navigator. Select a user to view their roles and permissions.

If you are not a system admin, navigate to Organizational Hierarchy > Hierarchy Tree > OU Members show the list of Tasker roles for a user. Please refer to additional information about rolesView Users by Roles and Tasker permissions. Table of Permissions, Administration, and Recommendations Table of Permissions, Administration, and Recommendations

23.      How do I configure and customize the portal landing page for my organization’s preference?

Portal configuration includes customizing the homepage/portal to your organization’s preferences, including your logo, appearance of menus, color schemes, and CSS.

https://docs.servicenow.com/bundle/newyork-servicenow-platform/page/build/service-portal/concept/c_BrandingEditor.html

 24.      What do I do if the application lags or I get an error toast message?

To troubleshoot performance, navigate to System Logs > Transactions to see response time, output length, and rule counts.

For error logs, navigate to System Logs > Errors to see timestamped error messages. Please refer to the appendix for additional troubleshooting recommendations.

25.      How do I implement an update set and roll back?

Please refer to section on update sets for direction to implement an update set. When you finish making a version of that update set, you can roll it back by selecting Rollback in the related links section. Implementing Update Sets

26.      How can I reduce the number of automated emails being sent?

You can manage your email preferences to limit the number of email notifications received by managing your email settings. Administrators can add unsubscribe links to notifications so that users can stop receiving particular email messages. Manage Email Notifications

27.      How do I know if email tasks are working?

Check your System Logs > Email to see what emails were received or ignored.

28.      How do I manage the type-ahead feature?

If users are reporting that the task search feature is not working, you need to manage the type-ahead feature. Go to the glide.service_portal.search_as_you_type_behavior system property and set the value of this property to Typeahead. This change will enable a user to see a list of search suggestions. For detailed information about this, please see the ServiceNow Documentation.

29.      How do I connect to SharePoint?

You must have the DocIntegrator application to get SharePoint content in your tasks and templates. DocIntegrator will work with Azure/O365 and SP2013 or later. See the sections for Azure or SP2013 DocIntegrator configuration. Configure DocIntegrator in Tasker (Azure/O365)Configure DocIntegrator in Tasker (SP On-Premise)

30.      How do I make a document clickable for DocIntegrator in Tasker?

When you are setting up your library views, you get the view fields and then customize the list to make a field clickable with a True or False value.

31.      What is the difference between a task and a template?

A task is a single one-time assignment whereas a template is a repeatable set of tasks.

32.      What is a task tree?

A task tree is a task with its branching sub tasks. You may build task trees with its parent tasks, sub tasks, and branching secondary and/or tertiary sub tasks. The top task is trunk of the task tree. The sub tasks are the arms of the trees. Each arm may have branches of sub tasks depending on how granular you wish the task tree to be.

33.      How do I know what OU or office I belong to?

Users can quickly find their assigned OU by selecting Organizational Hierarchy > My Offices. The user’s OU, title, and permissions will display.

34.      What is a Tasker OU member?

A person of your organization or team.

35.      What is the difference between a Tasking Authority, TA, TM, and a Task Owner?

A tasking authority represents the office that requested work. The Task Administrator (TA) is a power user role that may task offices or members. A Task Manager (TM) is able to task members within their office. The Task Owner is the user for whom the work is being done and the member or office that is managing the task.

36.      How do you move a user to a different OU?

Moving a user to a different OU requires hierarchy permissions, and is also dependent on where the user is moving in the hierarchy. To move a user to a different OU, you first must remove the user. Navigate to the Organizational Hierarchy and select the node of the OU. Remove the user and then add the user back into the appropriate OU.

37.      What roles and permissions are needed to change the organizational hierarchy?

The roles associated with organizational hierarchy permissions are Global Hierarchy Admin and OU Hierarchy Admin.

38.      How do you check the roles and permissions assigned to a Tasker user?

Navigate to Organizational Hierarchy > Hierarchy Tree > OU Members show the list of roles for a user. Please refer to Update Member Permissions. Update User Roles/Permissions

39.      How do I create a task?

From the Tasker Main Portal, select the New Task Shortcut or from the Application Navigator, enter New Task.

40.      How can I cancel or go back (button) when creating a task, sub task or template?

Sometimes you will need to cancel a task because of duplication, resource availability, scheduling parameters, and other circumstances. Select Cancel Task by clicking the context menu.

41.      How do I work a task?

You also can start work immediately after accepting the task. To work a task, you click Start Work. Remember that some tasks skip the work in progress state. Refer to Task Types for more information. Task Types and Lifecycles

42.      How do I reject a task?

Sometimes you will need to reject a task because of duplication, resource availability, scheduling parameters, and other circumstances. Please note that you must enter comments with a reason the task was rejected. A popup will alert you if you have missed this step. Select Reject Task.

43.      What happens when I reject task assignment?

When a user rejects a task assignment, the task’s state changes from assigned to rejected. The task is returned to TAs, TMs, and the task owner who can then reassign the task to an office or to a user.

44.      How do I request an extension for my task deadline?

While you are working a task, there is a request an extension button available. Please refer to request extension for more information. Request an Extension

45.      How do I approve a task or send for rework?

Task authorities and reviewers are responsible to review tasks, and then approve or send for rework. These options are available as buttons Mark Complete or Send for rework for standard tasks.

46.      Who can change the reviewer of a task?

Only the task owner and Task Administrators may change the reviewer of a task.

47.      How do I create a template?

Select New Template from the Tasker Portal to create a new template. You may also use the context menu so Save Task as Template.

48.      How do I run a template?

After creating the template and the template schedule, you are ready to run the template and its associated tasks. Click View/Run to continue. Click Run Template to continue. You will see a notification popup confirming that the template is running. Refer to run a template schedule for more information. You may also run a template from a task by selecting Run Template from the context menu. Run a Template

49.      What is shown on the Tasker Dashboard?

The Tasker dashboard consists of three tabs: Overview, Tasks by My Office and My Tasks. Each tab has reports with the ability to filter criteria. Tasker Dashboard and Reports

50.      How do I create a task from an email (suggested task)?

To create a task from an email, enter your ServiceNow instance into the To: line (e.g. ven0287) as shown below. Enter Task: [shortname] into the subjectline (e.g. Task: Submit Timesheets by Friday COB).Create a Suggested Task from an Email

51.      How do I request a new password?

Contact your ServiceNow IT administrator who can reset your password.

52.      What browsers work with the application?

The application works with the browsers support by the platform. ServiceNow works best in Google Chrome. Some functionality will not work in other browsers, particularly in Internet Explorer.

53.      Why do I see different banner notifications?

The banner notifications you see are based on the state of the task, and whether you are the task owner, task reviewer, etc.

54.      How do I know who is my Task Manager (TM)?

Go to the organizational hierarchy. In the OU Member Tab you can see a list of users and permissions, including who is the TM.

55.      Where do I see who is my TA?

Go to the organizational hierarchy. In the OU Member Tab you can see a list of users and permissions, including who is the TA.

56.      Who can approve my extension request?

Task owners and TMs accept or reject extensions. If your task has a parent task, the assignee of the parent task can approve. If your task does not have a parent task, the task owner can approve. From the task tree view, TAs have options to Reject Extension Request or Accept Extension Request.

57.      When does the actual start day get populated for a task?

The time starts when the user clicks Start Work.

58.      What is an active task?

After you click Start Work, the task is now active. The task remains active until it is marked as complete or is cancelled.

59.      My reviewer is no longer in the organization, who can review my task?

The Task Admin, Task Owner, Assigned Member, and On Behalf Of (member) may change the reviewer for the active task.

60.      Why do I see different workflows and approval types?

For standard tasks, tasks are accepted, started, and then move to work in progress. Non-standard tasks skip the work in progress state. Refer to task types and lifecycles for more information. Task Types and Lifecycles

61.      What do the priority and urgency selections mean?

These options are meant to assist the user in organizing tasks based on priorities and urgency, so that urgent prioritized tasks are completed on time.

62.      What is the difference between Tasking Authority, Primary Office, Primary Assignee in the Assignment section?

The Primary Office is the OU responsible for the entire task tree. The Tasking Authority OU requested the task.

If the Tasking Authority OU is an external OU, then an Internal OU needs to be selected for the Primary Office as that office is responsible for running the task tree through completion.

63.      Why can't I see Shared Documents?

If you cannot see Shared Documents, it is because the owner of the top task has disabled this feature or you do not have access to the DocIntegrator application. Check with your application administrator to verify you have the right DocIntegrator permissions and make sure you have the right permissions in SharePoint.

64.      Why can't I add an attachment?

Check to see if the owner of the top task has disabled attachments. Also note that the system only allows users to add attachments on active tasks.

65.      Why can't I see other OUs and members in the Task Assignment drop-downs?

A user's ability to assign a task is based on Permissions and the tasking rules for the Organization Settings. Permissions in TaskerTasking Rules for your Organization (System Setting)

66.      How do I customize and pull a report from the Tasker Dashboard?

Select the Dashboard shortcut from the Tasker homepage, and then use the report tabs and filters to narrow your search criteria. Follow the instructions to click the graphical element and export the list to the desired file extension. Pull a Dashboard Report

67.      Why do I see "suspense date must be in the future" error?

If setting the suspense date to today, you will receive a toast notification "suspense date must be in the future." This is caused by the default behavior that will set the time to the second it is set (which is already in the past by the time the user submits). As a workaround, click the clock icon on the suspense date calendar and set the time manually to the future before setting a date.
